title: Counting per group
prompt_md: How many bookings does each user have? Return `user_id` and the count aliased as `bookings`. Use `GROUP BY` and cast the count to int with `::int`.
starter_code: SELECT user_id, COUNT(*)::int AS bookings FROM bookings GROUP BY user_id;
hints: `GROUP BY user_id` collapses rows into one group per user.
`COUNT(*)::int AS bookings` counts rows in each group and names the column.
reference_solution: SELECT user_id, COUNT(*)::int AS bookings FROM bookings GROUP BY user_id;
